title: Heroes Around the World Day
story: Every August 19, people around the world celebrate World Humanitarian Day to honor amazing helpers who risk their lives to save others. These brave heroes work in dangerous places during wars, natural disasters, and emergencies to bring food, medicine, and hope to people who need it most. title: Sky-High Fun on National Aviation Day
story: Pilots, plane spotters, and aviation fans celebrate National Aviation Day every August 19! This special day honors the amazing world of flight and all the people who make it possible. Airports across the country host open houses where families can tour cockpits, watch air shows, and meet real pilots. Museums display vintage aircraft and let kids try flight simulators. Many people share photos of planes they've spotted or post about their favorite flying experiences on social media. Aviation clubs organize special events and some lucky visitors even get to take discovery flights in small aircraft. title: Turn Up the Heat Day!
story: Fire-breathing food lovers unite every August 19 for National Hot and Spicy Food Day! People celebrate by chomping down on the spiciest wings, hottest peppers, and most blazing dishes they can handle. Restaurants offer special spicy menu items and challenge customers to try their hottest creations. Some people host spicy cooking contests with friends and family, while others visit their favorite Mexican, Thai, or Indian restaurants to test their taste buds against seriously hot dishes.

Give away free ice cream to cool down

activity_answer: Offer special spicy menu items and challenges

He sent Eddie Gaedel, who stood just 3 feet 7 inches tall, to bat for the St. Louis Browns. Gaedel, wearing a tiny uniform with the number 1/8, stepped up to the plate. title: Vanishing Waters: The Aral Sea's Disappearance
story: Satellites high above Earth captured a startling image in 2014. The eastern basin of the Aral Sea, once a vast lake between Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan, had completely dried up. This marked a sad milestone for what was once the world's fourth-largest lake. Years of water diversion for farming had slowly shrunk the sea. On August 19, NASA released photos showing the dramatic change, highlighting the urgent need to protect our planet's precious water resources.

story: Cannons boomed as two mighty ships clashed on the Atlantic. The USS Constitution, a powerful American warship, faced off against the British HMS Guerriere.
